title: ConstructionAxes Object
description: Provides access to the construction axes within a component and provides methods to create new construction axes.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates and adds a new ConstructionAxis using the creation parameters in the ConstructionAxisInput.   If the ConstructionAxisInput was defined using the setByLine method then the add will only work in the direct modeling mode and will fail in the parametric modeling mode.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Create a ConstructionAxisInput object that is in turn used to create a ConstructionAxis.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified construction axis using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Returns the specified construction axis using the name of the construction axis as it is displayed in the browser.

properties.name: component
properties.description: The component that owns this collection.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of construction axes in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Construction Axis API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction axis in various ways.

add.title: ConstructionAxes.add Method
add.description: Creates and adds a new ConstructionAxis using the creation parameters in the ConstructionAxisInput. If the ConstructionAxisInput was defined using the setByLine method then the add will only work in the direct modeling mode and will fail in the parametric modeling mode.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created construction axis or null if the creation failed.

related_samples.name: Construction Axis API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction axis in various ways.

classType.title: ConstructionAxes.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: ConstructionAxes.createInput Method
createInput.description: Create a ConstructionAxisInput object that is in turn used to create a ConstructionAxis.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = constructionAxes_var.createInput()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = constructionAxes_var.createInput(occurrenceForCreation)

parameters.name: occurrenceForCreation
parameters.description: A creation occurrence is needed if the input is in another component AND the construction axis is not in the root component. The occurrenceForCreation is analogous to the active occurrence in the UI.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

related_samples.name: Construction Axis API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction axis in various ways.

item.title: ConstructionAxes.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified construction axis using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: ConstructionAxes.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Returns the specified construction axis using the name of the construction axis as it is displayed in the browser.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid name was specified.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the axis as it is displayed in the browser

component.title: ConstructionAxes.component Property
component.description: The component that owns this collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = constructionAxes_var.component

count.title: ConstructionAxes.count Property
count.description: The number of construction axes in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = constructionAxes_var.count

related_samples.name: Delete Empty Components
related_samples.description: Deletes empty components from the active design.

isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = constructionAxes_var.isValid

objectType.title: ConstructionAxes.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = constructionAxes_var.objectType
